overall_assessment: Blocked: RR-AUTH-04 is not closure-ready because the reset-token hardening is not concurrency-safe, the fail-closed OTP-first rule is still bypassable through landlord auth catalog configuration, and the TODO still records unresolved required audit-floor lanes.
recommended_path: Do not close RR-AUTH-04 yet. First make password-reset issue and consume semantics atomic under concurrent requests with regression coverage, then enforce and test phone_otp as the fail-closed fallback regardless of landlord catalog drift, and finally record the remaining required audit-floor artifacts or explicit waivers in the governing TODO.

findings.title: Password reset tokens are not robustly single-use under concurrent requests
findings.rationale: The hardening package claims single-use reset tokens, but the implementation still performs delete/insert on issue and read/check/delete on consume as separate operations. In /laravel-app/app/Application/Auth/PasswordResetTokenService.php, issueForUser deletes by user_id and then inserts a new record, while consumeForUser loads the record with first() and deletes it only after hash and expiry checks. Two overlapping issue requests can leave more than one valid token record, and two overlapping consume/reset requests can both validate the same token before either delete is observed. The bounded tests prove happy-path single use, but they do not cover this concurrency window.
findings.suggested_action: Make issuance and consumption atomic for a single user/token record, add a uniqueness invariant for the user token slot, and add concurrency regression coverage that proves the same token cannot be consumed twice and that overlapping issuance leaves only one valid token.

findings.title: The closure packet still lacks required audit-floor evidence
findings.rationale: The governing TODO and bounded package both still say RR-AUTH-04 has pending required audit-floor lanes beyond this final review. The TODO's Delivery-Side Audit Floor marks security review, verification-debt audit, test-quality audit, and triple audit as pending, and both the TODO and package say closure requires those artifacts or explicit waivers. As written, the delivery packet is not ready for final closure even if the code were otherwise accepted.
findings.suggested_action: Keep RR-AUTH-04 open until the required security, verification-debt, test-quality, and triple-audit artifacts are recorded in the TODO, or an explicit approval-authority waiver is attached for each omitted lane.

findings.title: OTP-first fail-closed behavior still depends on landlord catalog shape
findings.rationale: The replacement rule says empty or invalid tenant subsets must collapse to phone_otp, but the implementation only returns phone_otp when it is present in available_methods. In /laravel-app/app/Application/Auth/TenantPublicAuthMethodResolver.php, failClosedDefaultMethods falls back to the landlord-supplied available methods if phone_otp is absent. The landlord patch guard in /laravel-app/app/Integration/Settings/TenantPublicAuthMethodPatchGuard.php only enforces that available_methods is non-empty and supported, so a landlord configuration of ['password'] remains valid. Under that configuration, an empty or invalid tenant subset silently re-enables password, which contradicts the TODO/package claim that this slice freezes OTP-first fail-closed governance.
findings.suggested_action: Enforce phone_otp as a required landlord-available method for tenant-public auth or make the fail-closed fallback independent of available_methods, then add a regression test for a password-only landlord catalog.
